q: In a period, the properties of transition elements are very similar because the ___ shell electronic configuration of the elements does not change.
These transition elements are solid metals and have typical metallic properties such as ___, ductility, metallic lustre, and high tensile strength.
They are generally good conductors of ___ and heat.
One cubic centimetre ($1 \text{ cm}^{3}$) cube of iron is 8 times heavier than a $1 \text{ cm}^{3}$ cube of ___.
Transition elements are significantly less reactive than ___ metals.
Iron combined with non-metal carbon produces an alloy called ___.
Most transition elements form ___ compounds.
Transition elements show ___ oxidation states, and they can form more than one compound with another element.
Most transition elements can form ___ ions.
Transition elements typically have incompletely filled $d$ orbitals or readily give rise to ions with ___ $d$ orbitals.
Transition elements usually lose the electron from the ___ orbital first, which is followed by the removal of an electron from the $d$ orbitals.
The $+3$ oxidation states (i.e., $M^{3+}$ ions) are more stable at the ___ of the series.
The highest oxidation state is +7 for ___ ($_{25}Mn$).
The ions of chromium, manganese, and iron have the highest oxidation states, ___, +7, and +6, respectively.
These ions tend to be good ___ agents, e.g., dichromate ion ($Cr_{2}O_{7}^{2-}$), permanganate ion ($MnO_{4}^{-}$), and ferrate(VI) ion ($FeO_{4}^{2-}$).
Transition metals usually have their highest oxidation states in compounds with highly ___ elements such as oxygen and fluorine.
The colour of compounds of transition metals is due to the presence of ___ $d$ orbitals or the presence of unpaired electrons.
Transition metal compounds form coloured ions in an aqueous solution because they absorb part of the ___ light and transmit the complement colour.
The characteristic colour of transition metal ions is helpful when performing ___ analysis.
$Sc^{3+}$ and $Zn^{2+}$ ions are colourless because $Sc^{3+}$ ion has completely ___ $d$ orbitals.
If the electrons are unpaired then it is a ___ substance.
If the electrons are paired, then it is a ___ substance.
___ is a paramagnetic substance whereas copper and zinc are diamagnetic substances.
Substances that are attracted very strongly by the applied magnetic field are said to be ___.
Iron, cobalt, and nickel are ___ substances.
Transition metals are widely used at the industrial level due to their ___ properties.
Iron is used as a catalyst in the manufacture of ammonia (___ process).
Vanadium is used in the form of vanadium(V) oxide ($V_{2}O_{5}$) in the manufacture of sulphuric acid (___ process).
___ is used as a catalyst in the hydrogenation of alkenes.
Titanium(IV) chloride ($TiCl_{4}$) is used for the manufacturing of high-density ___ in industries.
Paramagnetic substance is a substance that has ___ and shows weak attraction towards the external magnetic field.
___ substance is a substance that has no unpaired electrons and shows no attraction (slight repulsion) from the external magnetic field.
Complex ion is an ion, which has a ___ at its centre with a number of other molecules or ions surrounding it.
